entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The crop being studied for the genetic basis of root morphology, nutrient uptake, and yield.

entities.name: DRY DIRECT-SEEDED RICE (DDSR)
entities.description: A cultivation system where rice is sown directly into dry soil, contrasting with puddled systems.

entities.name: PUDDLED RICE CULTIVATION
entities.description: A traditional rice cultivation method involving the puddling of soil before transplanting.

entities.name: GENOME-WIDE ASSOCIATION STUDY
entities.description: A method used to identify associations between genetic markers and phenotypic traits across the entire genome.

entities.name: MAPPING POPULATION
entities.description: A complex group of individuals used in genetic mapping to identify QTLs.

entities.name: SEEDLING-ESTABLISHMENT TRAITS
entities.description: Traits related to the initial growth and successful establishment of rice seedlings.

entities.name: ROOT MORPHOLOGY
entities.description: The physical structure and characteristics of the root system.

entities.name: NUTRIENT UPTAKE
entities.description: The process and efficiency of absorbing essential nutrients from the soil.

entities.name: PLANT MORPHOLOGICAL TRAITS
entities.description: Physical characteristics related to the overall structure of the plant.

entities.name: LODGING RESISTANCE TRAITS
entities.description: Traits that determine the ability of the plant to resist falling over.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The total amount of grain produced per unit area.

entities.name: YIELD-CONTRIBUTING TRAITS
entities.description: Specific traits that directly influence the final grain yield.

entities.name: MARKER-TRAIT ASSOCIATIONS (MTAS)
entities.description: Statistical associations between genetic markers and specific phenotypic traits.

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE TRAIT LOCI (QTLS)
entities.description: Regions of the genome associated with a particular quantitative trait.

entities.name: SINGLE NUCLEOTIDE POLYMORPHISMS (SNPS)
entities.description: Single base pair variations in the DNA sequence used as genetic markers.

entities.name: GENOMICS-ASSISTED BREEDING
entities.description: Breeding programs that utilize genomic information to improve crop traits and adaptability.
